tasks.label: Graded assignment — 90% done, submit when finished

memory.summary: I can finish this in minutes, but I'd rather grab a quick bite with my friends first. Why stress over the last 10% right now?
I missed the deadline by seconds because I was too scared to finish alone. Now my friends are probably waiting for me, and I just want to hide.
I finally pushed through the last bits, but my friends are probably still waiting. I hope they don't notice how stressed I look.
I'm almost done, but I'd rather just hang out with my friends than stress over the final 1% right now.

desires: Escape the immediate pressure of finishing this assignment to avoid feeling like a failure in front of my friends.
Reconnect with my friend group immediately to regain emotional safety and hide my stress.
Delay the final submission steps to prevent the anxiety of being judged for being 'late' or 'incomplete'.
Prioritize social validation over academic perfection, even if it means the work isn't truly done yet.

goals.description: Hit submit on the assignment immediately, even though it's technically late.
goals.rationale: My 'avoidant' trait is in overdrive. The deadline passed an hour ago, and every second I spend here increases the risk of the professor noticing my lateness or seeing my messy work. That creates interpersonal friction I can't handle. I need to get this 'threat' out of my face right now so I can stop feeling like a target.

goals.description: Run to meet cohort_3 for our meal immediately after hitting submit.
goals.rationale: Relationships are my absolute priority (0.5). Once I've submitted the work (even if imperfect), I need to physically be with my friends to feel safe and accepted. Being alone with this unfinished business feels isolating. I want their validation to wash away any lingering anxiety about the grade.

goals.description: Completely ignore the note tidying task until it becomes an immediate emergency.
goals.rationale: My short planning horizon means Friday feels like a lifetime away. Worrying about it now only adds stress that distracts from my friends. It has no impact on my relationships or current emotional state, so I'll just pretend it doesn't exist until someone yells at me.

agent.last_reasoning: Advances g1 (submit immediately) to eliminate the interpersonal threat of the professor seeing my messy work. I'm ignoring the note tidying (g3) because short-term stress relief is more valuable than future organization.

goals.description: Finish the final 10% of the assignment and submit it immediately to prevent a social awkwardness or conflict with the professor later.
goals.rationale: My 'avoidant' trait means I can't handle the stress of being late or facing a professor's disappointment, even though my 'impulsive' side usually ignores deadlines. Since the deadline is literally 28 minutes away (13:30), the immediate risk of conflict overrides my usual procrastination. Relationships with authority figures are high stakes for me.

goals.description: Join friends for a meal right after submitting to maintain closeness and ensure no one feels left out.
goals.rationale: Relationships are my top priority (0.5 weight). Leaving my friends alone would cause interpersonal friction, which I actively avoid. The 'affiliation' drive compels me to be present with them immediately after clearing the small task hurdle.

goals.description: Ignore the 'note tidying' task entirely for now.
goals.rationale: The deadline is Friday, so there is zero immediate pressure. My 'impulsive' nature and short planning horizon mean I will only deal with it if it becomes urgent, not because it's a good idea. It doesn't impact my relationships or current safety.

desires: Grab a quick bite with my friends to maintain closeness and avoid the awkwardness of leaving them alone
